name: Outeiro de Rei – return via A Feira de Monte and Torre de Caldaloba
name_original: Outeiro de Rei – vuelta por A Feira de Monte y Torre de Caldaloba
region_intro: Touring cycling around Rábade features a landscape characterized by river valleys, historical bridges, and varied terrain that includes both gentle slopes and more challenging ascents. The region offers a mix of rural paths and routes connecting small villages. Elevations range from low-lying areas near water bodies to higher points providing scenic viewpoints. Best touring cycling routes around Rábade The most popular touring cycling route is Outeiro de Rei – return via A Feira de Monte and Torre de Caldaloba, a 28.7 miles (46.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 56 minutes to complete. This route includes historical points and varied scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ponte Vella de Rábade – Mirador de Paraños loop from Igrexa de Santa María de Saavedra, a difficult 45.6 miles (73.4 km) path. This route features an old bridge and a viewpoint, offering expansive vistas. Local touring cyclists also love the Ombreiro Bridge loop from N-VI (Cereijo), a 12.5 miles (20.1 km) trail leading through areas around a significant bridge, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Rábade is defined by river crossings, historical bridges, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family friendly touring bike routes Rábade to more demanding trails. The routes in Rábade are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 30 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Rábade's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Rábade?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes in the Rábade area, offering a variety of experiences from easy rides to more challenging excursions. These routes explore the region's river valleys, historical bridges, and rural landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Rábade?
A: Yes, Rábade offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Os Castros loop from O Monte de Abaixo&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 8.5 km (5.3 miles) trail that can be completed in about 30 minutes, featuring minimal elevation changes.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on touring cycling routes in Rábade?
A: Touring cyclists in Rábade can expect diverse scenery, including picturesque river valleys, historical bridges, and rural paths connecting small villages. The landscape features both gentle slopes and some more challenging ascents, offering expansive vistas from higher points. You'll often ride alongside the Miño River and through areas rich in natural beauty.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Rábade?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Rábade are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40332559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ombreiro Bridge loop from N-VI (Cereijo)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 20.1 km (12.5 miles) trail that takes about 1 hour 33 minutes, offering a convenient circular option.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while cycling in Rábade?
A: The Rábade area is rich in historical and natural landmarks. Along your rides, you might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Vella de Rábade&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caldaloba Tower&lt;/a&gt;, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penas de Rodas Rock Formation&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1043413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Miño in Reserva da Biosfera Terras do Miño&lt;/a&gt; also offers beautiful natural sights.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Rábade?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Rábade is generally during the spring and autumn months when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be warm, while winters are typically cooler and wetter, though still offering opportunities for cycling on clearer days.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by lakes or rivers in Rábade?
A: Yes, Rábade's landscape is characterized by its rivers and natural water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boots of Begonte Sculpture – Riocaldo Lagoons loop from Igrexa de Santa María de Saavedra&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, taking you past the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riocaldo Lagoons&lt;/a&gt; and offering views of the Miño River.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Rábade?
A: The touring cycling routes around Rábade are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the historical points of interest, and the beautiful river views.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Rábade offers more demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31881908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Vella de Rábade – Mirador de Paraños loop from Igrexa de Santa María de Saavedra&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 73.4 km (45.6 miles) path that includes significant elevation changes and takes nearly 6 hours to complete, featuring expansive vistas from the Mirador de Paraños.

Q: Can I find routes that offer scenic viewpoints in the Rábade area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Rábade are designed to showcase the region's natural beauty, including several scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31881908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Vella de Rábade – Mirador de Paraños loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads to the Mirador de Paraños, offering panoramic views. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3172743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Miño River from the Hombreiro Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is a popular spot for scenic appreciation.

Q: Are there options for less crowded touring cycling routes in Rábade?
A: While Rábade's routes are generally not overly crowded, to find quieter experiences, consider exploring some of the less-trafficked rural paths or venturing out during off-peak hours. Routes that connect smaller villages often provide a more serene cycling environment away from main roads.
tips: A stone bridge with 9 arches was built over a network of paths during medieval times. Like many bridges in Galicia, the original structure could have been built by the Romans.
Lush green forested area on the banks of the Mino River, the Santa Isabel Recreational Zone is a great place to explore.
The river laps numerous islands which provide habitats for many trees, birds and other animal species.
Ruins of a defensive fortress masonry watchtower near Cospeito, dating from the medieval period.
